element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Player learns overhead perspective and basic movement before any combat
Collected passively during normal exploration; foundation for upgrade system

element_introduction_order.element: Small hostile sea creatures (jellyfish, rays, small sharks)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Player learns side-view combat mechanics in relatively low-threat encounters
Basic weapon system introduced through forced diving sequence
Receiver device unlocks proximity tracking; subsequent visits increase attack power
Critical tool for locating Jaws; beep frequency indicates distance
First Jaws collision triggers side-view combat with multiple phases
Temporary upgrade with torpedoes and depth charges; fragile but powerful

element_introduction_order.first_appears: After killing specific number of smaller sharks
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Reward sequence with Galaga-like mechanics; conch shell rewards
Player attacks Jaws with depth charges; diver combat variant
Timing-critical final mechanic; player must breach Jaws and ram at correct moment

typical_relief_triggers: Killing small sharks without Jaws intervention
typical_tension_triggers: Collision with boat on overhead map (forced into side-view)
Encountering hostile sea creatures during diving
Jaws appears on map/collides with boat
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Cyclical tension builds through proximity detection (beeping intensity), escalates during forced encounters, releases through safe port visits and minigame rewards. Late game frontloads difficulty with final three-phase Jaws encounter requiring timing mastery.

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Submarine pickups appear at random locations on map; players may never encounter them without deliberate exploration
spatial_grammar.secret_convention: Beeping proximity device provides subtle audio cue for danger direction; faster beep = closer threat

difficulty_curve.early_game: Safe overhead navigation with introduction to conch collection and port system. First diving encounters feature weak creatures (jellyfish, rays) that teach combat basics without lethal threat
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Increased frequency of small shark encounters and boat collisions. Jaws begins appearing with receiver device installed. Player must balance resource gathering (conch shells) with upgrade progression. Random submarine pickups provide brief power spikes
difficulty_curve.late_game: Jaws dominates encounters. Players face mandatory side-view combat with significant power drain on death (lose tracking device, lose half conch shells, lose power level). Final sequence shifts to first-person perspective with strict timing requirements for boat ramming
notable_spikes: First Jaws encounter (immediate difficulty spike from small creatures)
Loss of tracking device after death (removes safety information)
Final first-person ramming phase (requires precision timing; described as 'quite difficult' by sources)

notable_level_patterns.description: Overhead map navigation (safe) → boat collision (transition) → side-view diving (moderate danger) → Jaws appearance (high danger). Each zone shift increases threat level and changes perspective/mechanics
Navigate overhead map to collect conch shells → encounter small creatures → return to port → upgrade → repeat. Loop continues until sufficient power to face Jaws
After killing specific shark count threshold, bonus airplane stage plays (Galaga-style jellyfish encounter). Provides relief from tension and conch shell rewards without risk of resource loss

Jaws battle splits into three distinct phases: (1) Side-view depth charge combat, (2) Health recovery, (3) First-person strobe-and-ram finale. Each phase requires different mechanic mastery and offers multiple failure points
